desires: Secure the scholarship money immediately to avoid future financial anxiety, even if it means sacrificing sleep tonight.
Avoid the immediate social friction of explaining my procrastination or lack of progress to teammates tomorrow.

goals.description: Immediately launch the 'fun_now' game stream to secure instant dopamine and escape the mounting guilt of the untouched application.
goals.rationale: My impulsive nature and short planning horizon dictate immediate gratification. The deadline is three days away, so I am discounting the future consequence of rushing later. Avoiding the mental friction of the essay right now is the most efficient way to manage current stress.

goals.description: Maintain strict isolation from hallmates; decline any greetings or invitations to ensure a zero-interaction environment.
goals.rationale: Interpersonal friction drains my focus capacity. Even though I'm not working on the application yet, social interaction is an unnecessary energy expenditure that conflicts with my avoidant trait and need for a low-effort state.

goals.description: Reframe the procrastination as 'strategic rest' to preserve self-image while delaying the scholarship application until tomorrow.
goals.rationale: While I want to minimize effort, actively justifying the delay requires cognitive load I don't have right now. I will simply act as if it's a break without needing to construct a narrative until the guilt becomes unmanageable later.
